item.title: Moving from iTerm to Alacritty
item.description: This post is a part of the Project Restore, with the primary aim of excavating my previous writings. The material contained herein may not hold practical relevance and is most likely completely useless. A few years ago I decided to use Vim as my text editor. It has been a fantastic journey so far. I have learned a lot about Vim and I keep learning new things every day. This continuous learning makes the tool so interesting.

item.title: My Visual Studio Code Setup
item.description: This post is a part of the Project Restore, with the primary aim of excavating my previous writings. The material contained herein may not hold practical relevance and is most likely completely useless. When Microsoft announced Visual Studio Code in 2015, I did not have good things to say about it. However, times have changed and Visual Studio Code is my preferred code editor these days. The editor has come a long way and it is commendable that Microsoft is serious about maintaining VS Code.

item.title: Path Picker by Facebook
item.description: This post is a part of the Project Restore, with the primary aim of excavating my previous writings. The material contained herein may not hold practical relevance and is most likely completely useless. Path Picker is a little utility brought to us by Facebook. According to the official website PathPicker accepts a wide range of input – output from git commands, grep results, searches – pretty much anything. On a mac Path Picker can be installed by using brew.

item.title: Advanced Debugging Techniques with Chrome
item.description: This is a must watch video to see how good chrome debugging tools are. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=B63jNjSVEbQ

item.title: git log basic to advance
item.description: This post is a part of the Project Restore, with the primary aim of excavating my previous writings. The material contained herein may not hold practical relevance and is most likely completely useless. Git is just awesome and more awesome are the tools it comes with. Git log is one of those tools I use extensively throughout my day. I started using git log in its vanilla form and then slowly as my understanding of git increased, I started using fancier git log commands.

item.title: Vim update after using it for more than a month
item.description: This post is a part of the Project Restore, with the primary aim of excavating my previous writings. The material contained herein may not hold practical relevance and is most likely completely useless. It has now been close to a month since I started using Vim as my main editor. This post is about my Vim adventures. Lately I have found watching videos to be an excellent source of learning new things.

item.title: Starting Vim Journey Again
item.description: This post is a part of the Project Restore, with the primary aim of excavating my previous writings. The material contained herein may not hold practical relevance and is most likely completely useless. Every few months I talk myself into using Vim as my text editor. Mostly it happens after I have watched a presentation in which the presenter wows me with their code editing skills on Vim. I miss the main subject of the presentation because I get so wowed by their Vim ninja skills.

item.title: Free Icons And Images With Visual Studio 2008
item.description: This post is a part of the Project Restore, with the primary aim of excavating my previous writings. The material contained herein may not hold practical relevance and is most likely completely useless. Visual Studio 2008 comes with an Image Library which contains thousands of icons, animations and images which you can use in your applications. You can find the Image Library at C:Program FilesMicrosoft Visual Studio 9.0Common7VS2008ImageLibrary1033 Just unzip VS2008ImageLibrary.
